key_insight: PHO-Agents combines structured EHR modeling with LLM-based multi-agent reasoning to improve health outcome prediction and explanation quality.
clinical_relevance: For cancer patients on immune checkpoint inhibitors, the system aims to better flag immune-related adverse events and support more interpretable decision support.
method: Encodes longitudinal EHR sequences into attention-based patient representations and initial prediction logits.
Uses a data agent to turn EHR signals into patient summaries, a retrieval agent to fetch relevant clinical guidelines, and research/doctor agents to analyze the case.
A leader agent synthesizes agent outputs, then fuses LLM reasoning with EHR model logits for final predictions and explanation reports.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on three real-world cohorts and showed strong gains over baselines, including a PR-AUC of 90.20 ± 2.07 for AKI mortality versus 56.46 ± 2.98 for the best single-agent baseline.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable guideline retrieval and multi-agent LLM outputs, so performance and explainability may vary with data quality, prompt design, and deployment setting.
paper_title: PHO-Agents: A Large Language Model Powered Multi-Agent System for Predicting Health Outcomes
